title: chore(sops): split CI age key from team dev key
author: ElliotEtag

gist.headline: New keys for the robot
gist.blurb: Gave our automated deployment robots their very own set of keys. Better security boundaries mean a safer hive for everyone!
gist.summary: Separated the CI deployment identity from the shared developer key in SOPS to improve security boundaries.
gist.impact: Improves infrastructure security by ensuring automated systems use dedicated, isolated credentials.
gist.visual_concept: The pollinations bee (Polly) giving a shiny new key to the Monitor Robot (representing CI/CD). They are standing in front of a secure vault or a server rack.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot handing a shiny golden key to a chunky robot with a CRT monitor head. They are standing in a cozy server room with glowing lime green (#ecf874) lights on the racks. Soft pastel gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.

image.prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot handing a shiny golden key to a chunky robot with a CRT monitor head.
